Perfect! I found the information. The recommended ratio for diluting tea tree oil with a carrier oil is **1:10** (1 part tea tree oil to 10 parts carrier oil).

You should mix a few drops of tea tree oil with a carrier oil such as coconut oil, jojoba oil, or almond oil in this 1:10 ratio before applying it to your skin. This dilution is important because undiluted tea tree oil is too strong and can cause skin irritation, redness, itching, or even acne breakouts.

It's also a good idea to do a patch test first by applying a small amount of the diluted mixture to your forearm or another inconspicuous area to make sure you don't have any adverse reactions before using it on your face or other areas.
